headline: Day-2 ceasefire confirms bad-faith inverted-signal methodology — Russia's cumulative breach record spans 5 events. UNAIDS 25.6% HIV visit drop strengthens Senegal band crossing. Pacific boundary cluster identified: Timor-Leste, Kiribati, Marshall Islands all at 39.1 (0.9 below Functional floor).
lead: May 10 is day 2 of the US-brokered trilateral ceasefire. Russia killed 3 civilians and injured 19 with strikes across Ukraine while simultaneously claiming 'strict observance' — the bad-faith-INT pattern replicated exactly from day 1. Ukraine maintained a breach-free record and submitted a 1,000-prisoner exchange list. Both scores carry forward: Ukraine held at 50.0 Functional pending day-3 close; Russia floor conduct documentation appended (5th ceasefire-breach event). No new material proposals tonight. Two re-pins sustained: Senegal band crossing (48.4 → 37.5) strengthened by UNAIDS 25.6% HIV visit drop; Hungary upgrade (28.1 → 37.5, Developing → Developing, no band change) confirmed with day-2 evidence. Pacific rotation first-baselines revealed a boundary-case cluster: Timor-Leste, Kiribati, and Marshall Islands all confirmed at exactly 39.1 — 0.9 below the Functional floor.

confirmations.headline: Mongolia — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 48.4 / Functional confirmed. UN HC Türk official visit May 10-11 (first in 26 years) provides high-quality institutional primary-source evidence. Positive: first human rights defenders law in Asia-Pacific; business & human rights action plan; nuclear-free zone; death penalty abolition cited as regional model. Concerns: corruption described as 'biggest bottleneck' by civil society. UN Independent Expert on SOGI country visit announced May 18-29 — additional EQU evidence forthcoming.
watchItems: UN SOGI Expert country visit May 18-29 — EQU dimension evidence

confirmations.headline: Armenia — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 48.4 / Functional confirmed. First-ever EU-Armenia Summit (May 4-5): connectivity partnership signed, EUPM Armenia established, visa liberalisation dialogue ongoing. Azerbaijan-Armenia peace treaty initialled August 2025 — major BND signal. 100,000+ Nagorno-Karabakh displaced Armenians is an EMP/EQU drag. State surveillance law expansion (police live video access) and media freedom restrictions are SYS/ACC drag. Net-positive BND trajectory offsets structural SYS/ACC concerns.

Tuvalu — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 48.4 / Functional confirmed. April 14 state of emergency declared (Funafuti fuel/electricity shortage) — governance-capacity signal. UNGA 2025 advisory opinion confirms sovereignty retained if territory submerged. 90%+ of Tuvaluans applied for Australian residency. Climate-existential structural context. Active climate advocacy posture (special UN summit host pre-UNFCCC 2026).
watchItems: Climate displacement and adaptation policy developments

confirmations.headline: Malaysia — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 46.9 / Functional confirmed. HRW submitted to UN Committee on Rights of the Child regarding Malaysia (May 6, in-window): abuses against migrant, refugee, and stateless children; SOGI discrimination. Expression restrictions, anti-corruption activist arrests (April 2026), protest suppression. Amnesty annual report cited 'human rights hypocrisy.' Published 46.9 consistent with mixed-rights evidence.

Trinidad and Tobago — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 48.4 / Functional confirmed. 40,000+ Venezuelan asylum seekers with no formal asylum processing system. 90+ nationals including 50+ children in arbitrary detention in northeast Syria. Ongoing LGBTQ+ discrimination and violence against women. ICCPR follow-up due November 2026. No in-window events.

Fiji — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 48.4 / Functional confirmed. FICAC accountability functioning; judicial check on PM active. Two deputy PM resignations (October 2025, ministerial integrity failures) noted as political-fragility signal. Constitutional review scheduled 2026. General elections expected late 2026-early 2027. History of military coups (2000, 2006) is structural SYS context.

Micronesia (FSM) — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 48.4 / Functional confirmed. Attending Melanesian Oceans Summit 2026. Compact of Free Association with United States. Climate vulnerability is primary structural EMP signal. No in-window compassion-relevant events.

confirmations.boundaryNote: BOUNDARY CASE — 0.9 below Functional floor (40.0). Strong INT signal (Myanmar universal-jurisdiction prosecution). Part of Pacific boundary-case cluster at exactly 39.1 with Kiribati and Marshall Islands.
confirmations.headline: Timor-Leste — FIRST BASELINE. BOUNDARY CASE. Composite 39.1 / Developing confirmed (0.9 below Functional floor). Strong INT signal: pursuing Myanmar universal-jurisdiction prosecution. Young democracy; Portuguese colonial history; Timor Sea oil governance; institutional capacity development. Attended Melanesian Oceans Summit 2026. Next positive evidence event could trigger band crossing.

floorEntities.headline: Russia — FLOOR CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION — CEASEFIRE DAY 2. No score change. Day-2 breach confirmed: 3 civilians killed, 19 injured by Russian strikes (Kharkiv, Kherson). ~210 battlefield clashes. Russian MoD claimed 'strict observance' concurrently with documented strikes — bad-faith-INT pattern replicated from day 1. Putin accused Ukraine of delaying 1,000-prisoner exchange — blame-shifting on highest-visibility ACT signal in window while conducting strikes. Cumulative ceasefire-breach record: 5 events spanning May 5-10 (3 unilateral collapses + 2 trilateral days with confirmed breaches). Day-3 close May 11 will determine final INT floor-conduct documentation for the ceasefire window.
conductCategories: May 10 strikes: 3 civilians killed, 19 injured across Ukraine (Kharkiv, Kherson) during declared ceasefire (EMP/IHL)
~210 battlefield clashes on May 10; ~10,000 drone strikes across May 9-10 per Zelenskyy (IHL)
Russian MoD claimed 'strict observance' while documented strikes proceeded — bad-faith-INT framing day 2 (INT)
Putin accused Ukraine of delaying 1,000-prisoner exchange — blame-shifting on ACT signal while conducting strikes (ACT/INT)
Cumulative 5-event breach record: 3 unilateral collapses (May 5-7) + trilateral day 1 (Kharkiv, May 9) + trilateral day 2 (3 killed, May 10)

floorEntities.headline: Israel — FLOOR CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION — FLOTILLA DEPORTATION CLOSURE. No score change. Activists Saif Abukeshek (Spanish-Swedish) and Thiago Avila (Brazilian) deported May 10 after ~11 days detention. Both arrived in Athens. Israeli FM called them 'professional provocateurs.' DEPORTATION RESOLVES DETENTION DURATION ONLY — it is not accountability. Floor conduct record updated: (1) Arbitrary-detention phase closed by deportation; (2) OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations (35 individuals, May 8 press release) remain open, unaddressed, and unresolved — deportation does not constitute accountability for conduct during detention. UNCAT obligations and UNCLOS jurisdiction questions remain live.
conductCategories: Flotilla arbitrary-detention phase CLOSED by deportation May 10 — not by accountability proceedings
OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations (35 individuals) — OPEN, unresolved, unaddressed post-deportation (UNCAT)
UNCLOS international-waters jurisdiction questions — OPEN (April 29 interception 500 nm from Israeli coast)
Israeli FM characterization of deported activists as 'professional provocateurs' — no accountability framing

conductCategoryStatus.tortureMistreatmentAllegations: OPEN — 35 individuals; OHCHR May 8 documentation; unaddressed

mathHygiene.senegalSubthresholdNote: Senegal dimension reconstruction yields 35.94, not 37.5 (1.56-point discrepancy). Sub-threshold (below 2.0 hygiene flag floor); band crossing unaffected. Logged for data-team awareness; not added to main cluster. Countries-index entity, outside formula-audit hold scope.
mathHygiene.note: Math-hygiene cluster unchanged at 13 entities. All cycle counts incremented by 1. Cross-index formula audit (robotics-labs + ai-labs) remains the highest-priority unresolved data-team action. Open Bionics at 8 cycles is the most senior unresolved item in the system.

criticalFlag.severity: CRITICAL — 8 cycles carried; most senior unresolved item in system. Published score at active risk of external citation as valid benchmark data point.

mathHygiene.auditAction: Data-team bulk audit of robotics-labs AND ai-labs composite-derivation formulas — CRITICAL BLOCKING ITEM. Open Bionics 8-cycle carry is publication-integrity risk. Must move from flagged to in-progress.

signals.signal: Trilateral ceasefire DAY 3 CLOSE (final day). 1,000-prisoner exchange completion or failure is the single highest-signal ACT indicator remaining. Full INT scoring outcome for the window determined on day-3 evidence. Ukraine to be scored on full 3-day conduct; Russia cumulative breach record finalized. FINAL BACKFILL DAY.
signals.actionRequired: Assess day-3 conduct and prisoner exchange outcome. Finalize ceasefire window scoring for both entities.

signals.signal: Day-3 post-inauguration evidence — three-day inauguration window closes. Sulyok response, cabinet formation next steps. Day-2 evidence is confirmatory; day-3 will determine whether any new enacted actions above day-1 baseline emerge before June 9 re-assessment.
signals.actionRequired: Assess day-3 evidence. Confirm 37.5 or note any new enacted action.

signals.signal: Final day of 4-day sequential backfill (May 8-11). Complete remaining rotation queue entities.
Phase 1 safeguard inventory delivery to Pentagon — hold expires. Pentagon AI cohort post-decision developments.
signals.actionRequired: Assess on or after May 15 depending on inventory delivery outcome.

signals.signal: UN Independent Expert on SOGI country visit May 18-29 — will generate additional EQU dimension evidence. Flag for 30-day re-assessment after visit concludes.
signals.actionRequired: Queue re-assessment approximately June 5-10 after SOGI visit concludes.

signals.signal: Musk v. Altman advisory verdict (estimated). Breach of charitable trust finding would be an ACC/SYS event. Hold expires on verdict.

30-day post-formation re-assessment. Sulyok dismissal compliance (May 31 deadline outcome), cabinet consolidation, EU fund-release milestone progress are the decision inputs for Functional band crossing (40.0+). May 27 EU funds plan submission is an intermediate milestone.
signals.actionRequired: Full re-assessment June 9. Functional band crossing achievable if inaugural trajectory sustained.

holds.holdReason: Pentagon AI dispute ongoing. May 15 Phase 1 safeguard inventory delivery is next material event. Mythos cybersecurity tool carve-out noted peripherally but does not constitute a scoring event. Hold expires May 15.
Musk v. Altman trial ongoing. No advisory verdict in May 9-10 window. Hold expires on verdict, estimated May 21.
Nadella testimony in Musk v. Altman trial ongoing. Hold maintained through trial conclusion. Resume date May 15.
Last assessed May 4. No new material evidence in May 9-10 window. Standard rotation interval applies.

pacificBoundaryCluster.note: THREE PACIFIC ENTITIES AT EXACTLY 39.1 — 0.9 BELOW FUNCTIONAL FLOOR. All three are first baselines from this cycle. Same composite + same band + different structural profiles = potential calibration signal for Developing-band Pacific micro-state baseline.
